_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about significant beliefs and values of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples that shaped — and continue to shape — their communities.

_source.w: We are learning about significant beliefs and values of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples that shaped — and continue to shape — their communities.
y: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander beliefs and values are living systems that continue to guide communities today.
Understanding these beliefs and values builds respect for the spiritual and philosophical depth of Indigenous cultures.
These systems offer insights into sustainable living, community responsibility, and connection to place that are relevant to all of us.
eg: Exploring the concept of the Dreaming and how it connects past, present, and future.
Investigating beliefs about responsibility to Country — caring for land, water, animals, and plants.
Examining the values of kinship, reciprocity, and collective responsibility that underpin community life.
Discussing how spiritual beliefs guided laws, governance, and conflict resolution.
Analysing how these beliefs and values have been maintained and adapted in contemporary Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities.
Comparing Indigenous Australian belief systems with those of other First Nations peoples around the world.
